title: Fix sessions_new migration batch error on hub sync

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-sessions-migration
comments.content: Found root cause: db.rs:137 queries 'SELECT COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) FROM pragma_user_version' but the column name in pragma_user_version is 'user_version', not 'version'. The query always fails with 'no such column', unwrap_or(0) returns 0, and the entire migration block runs on every Database::open(). This causes the v7 sessions_new migration to re-run every time, failing because sessions already has 7 columns (from v8/v9) but sessions_new only has 5.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-sessions-migration
comments.content: Plan: (1) Fix the column name in the version query from 'version' to 'user_version'. (2) Make the v7 migration idempotent by dropping sessions_new if it exists before recreating it, and using explicit column names in the INSERT. (3) Clean up sessions_new in a new migration step.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-sessions-migration
comments.content: Decision: Fix the column name (primary fix) AND make v7 migration robust (defensive fix). The column name fix prevents re-running migrations on every open. The v7 robustness fix ensures that even if the migration does re-run (e.g., from a DB at user_version < 7), it handles the case where sessions has extra columns. Also dropping leftover sessions_new table.
